questions: For Step 4 (useModalImageHandlers): Should the modal support image duplication (onImageDuplicate)? It requires pendingFramePositions state which adds complexity. If not needed, we can omit it (the prop is optional in the interface).

For FinalVideoSection's onApplySettingsFromTask: Should clicking 'apply settings from task' in the modal actually update the shot's generation settings? This would modify the form values in the Generation Settings section — confirming this is desired behavior.
Should the Shot Images editor in the modal support the 'by-pair' generation mode, or just batch/timeline?

For shot workflow actions (onAddToShot, onCreateShot, onNewShotFromSelection): Should these work in the modal context, or can we leave them as noops? They seem oriented toward multi-shot management which may not make sense inside a single-shot modal.

success_criteria.criterion: Final Video section renders above Shot Images section in the accordion
Final Video section only renders when the shot has video outputs (useVideoOutputs returns non-empty data)
ShotImagesEditor mode toggle switches between batch and timeline views
readOnly is removed from both ShotImagesEditor and FinalVideoSection
Image reorder, delete, file drop, and generation drop operations work in the modal and persist to the database
All existing tests in VideoGenerationModalSections.test.tsx pass (updated as needed)
No regressions in the broader travel-between-images test suite
Lightbox and preview dialogs open correctly on top of the VideoGenerationModal
useModalImageHandlers hook is under ~100 lines, composing existing mutation hooks rather than reimplementing logic
No new dependencies on ShotSettingsContext or ShotEditorState — modal handlers are self-contained
Image upload via drag-and-drop works in the modal
Manual smoke test: open modal for shot with videos, verify all sections interactive

assumptions: Option B (use existing mutation hooks directly) is the right approach — not Option A (build from scratch in controller) or Option C (wrap with ShotSettingsProvider). The existing hooks like useRemoveImageFromShot, useAddImageToShot, useHandleExternalImageDrop are standalone React Query mutations that don't depend on ShotEditor state.
Shot workflow actions (onAddToShot, onCreateShot, onNewShotFromSelection) can remain as no-ops in the modal context since the modal is scoped to a single shot.
Structure video and audio handlers can be omitted (they're optional in the ShotImagesEditorEditActions interface) since the modal doesn't need those advanced editing features.
The 'by-pair' generation mode is not needed in the modal — only batch and timeline.
useVideoOutputs from useShotImages.ts is the right hook for checking video availability — it filters shot generations to video outputs and is a lightweight query.
Radix UI Dialog portals handle nested modals (lightbox on top of VideoGenerationModal) without additional configuration.
